warnings: Non-blocking observability semantics gap: scripts/release/plan_to_issues.py sets planned_issue_post_create_fetch ok to true whenever the post-create refetch returns at least one issue. If created_count is greater than refetched_count but refetched_count is non-zero, the mismatch is visible in the counts but ok remains true. This preserves the existing non-blocking partial-refetch behavior, so it is not a hard red-line failure for Issue #117.
validation: Review used only local repository files, local command output, and the provided issue text.

No release gate weakening found: the post-create empty-refetch path still raises RuntimeError after emitting the new event when enabled.
No secret leakage or secret logging found in the changed code or docs reviewed.
No publish, tag, package publish, or PyPI push logic modified.
No key tests deleted; new observability tests were added.

Behavior has local evidence: sdk/python/.venv/bin/pytest tests/observability/test_events.py -q passed with 10 tests; sdk/python/.venv/bin/python -m compileall scripts/observability scripts/release/plan_to_issues.py passed; sdk/python/.venv/bin/python -m scripts.release.plan_to_issues --dry-run --emit-events --milestone v1.6.2 emitted the structured planned_issue_post_create_fetch JSON line.
No uncertain external dependency introduced; the new helper uses Python standard library JSON/stdout behavior and existing local release automation paths.
residual_risks: The narrow validation does not run the full release gate or exercise live GitHub CLI issue creation/refetch.
Partial post-create refetch cardinality is not explicitly tested; counts expose the condition, but ok remains true for any non-empty refetch.
The dry-run evidence command emits the event shape without a plan file and without exercising parse_plan.
